edges.description: Persistent immune activation sustains bowel-wall inflammation with epithelial and stromal injury in affected segments.
Chronic mucosal immune activation promotes sustained IL-23-driven Th17 polarization.
Dysbiosis challenges epithelial barrier integrity and promotes abnormal host-microbiota interactions.
Altered gut microbial communities shape a pathological mucosal immune response.
Loss of Paneth cell granule secretion reduces antimicrobial peptide levels in intestinal lumen.
Loss of antimicrobial peptide defense permits microbial encroachment at the epithelial surface and increased translocation.
Increased microbial and luminal antigen exposure drives chronic mucosal immune activation.
Inflamed intestinal mucosa and epithelial injury perturb absorptive and secretory function, contributing to diarrheal symptoms.
Active intestinal inflammation causes abdominal pain during Crohn flares.
Persistent inflammatory injury promotes fibroblast activation and progressive tissue remodeling.
LRRK2-hyperactive macrophages release pro-inflammatory cytokines that disrupt Paneth cell autophagy and antimicrobial function.
Defective bacterial clearance and cytokine release amplify chronic intestinal inflammation.
Progressive stricture formation narrows the bowel lumen and produces obstructive symptoms.
TL1A-DR3 signaling amplifies pathogenic T-cell cytokine programs within the broader Crohn inflammatory network.
Activated T cells produce chemokines that recruit macrophages to perianal inflammatory sites.
edges.target: Myeloid Cell Recruitment to Perianal Tissue

edges.description: Recruited macrophages interact with resident fibroblasts, driving tissue remodeling.
edges.source: Myeloid Cell Recruitment to Perianal Tissue

edges.description: Chronic myeloid-fibroblast crosstalk and tissue remodeling drive perianal fistula and abscess phenotypes.

edges.source: Primary human small-intestinal monolayer barrier model
Primary human small-intestinal monolayer barrier model

evidence.reference_title: Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: The most frequent symptoms are abdominal pain and diarrhoea, which can seriously affect patients' quality of life.

evidence.reference_title: Chronic Abdominal Pain: Gastroenterologist Approach.
evidence.snippet: In Crohn's disease, inflammation causes pain.

evidence.reference_title: Pain Characteristics in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Monocentric Cross-Sectional Study.
evidence.snippet: The prevalence of pain was high in IBD patients ... and higher in CD patients.

evidence.reference_title: Distinct clinical phenotypes for Crohn's disease derived from patient surveys.
evidence.snippet: Using the patients'' self-reported information, we identified two subpopulations of Crohn's disease; these subpopulations differ in disease severity, associations with smoking, and genetic transmission patterns.

nodes.description: Reduced secretion of antimicrobial peptides (defensins, lysozyme) allows increased bacterial translocation across the intestinal epithelium.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Patients may experience diarrhea, abdominal pain, fever, weight loss, abdominal masses, and anemia.

evidence.reference_title: Weight loss from diagnosis of Crohn's disease to one year post-diagnosis results in earlier surgery.
evidence.snippet: Malnutrition might play a key role in the prognosis of patients with Crohn's disease (CD) ... Forty-one patients (24.8%) had body weight loss whereas 124 patients (75.2%) had no body weight loss.

nodes.description: The immune system attacks the gastrointestinal tract, leading to chronic inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Several factors have been implicated in the cause of Crohn's disease, including a dysregulated immune system, an altered microbiota, genetic susceptibility and environmental factors, but the cause of the disease remains unknown.

evidence.reference_title: The landscape of immune dysregulation in Crohn's disease revealed through single-cell transcriptomic profiling in the ileum and colon.
evidence.snippet: Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic gastrointestinal disease that is increasing in prevalence worldwide. CD is multifactorial, involving the complex interplay of genetic, immune, and environmental factors... we mapped markers of disease-associated myofibroblast activation and identified CHMP1A, TBX3, and RNF168 as regulators of fibrotic complications.

evidence.reference_title: Role of the lymphatic system in the pathogenesis of Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: The lymphatic system is re-emerging as a critical player in inflammatory and immune processes... Recent studies reporting lymphangitis, lymphangiogenesis, bacterial infiltration and lymph node infection, immune cell trafficking, and fat-wrapping in Crohn's disease suggest altered lymph drainage and lymphatic pumping, implicating the lymphatic system as a likely player in inflammatory disorders and IBDs.

experimental_models.description: Captures macrophage cytokine responses to proinflammatory signals within a human intestinal tissue context.
experimental_models.name: PSC-derived intestinal organoid-macrophage coculture model

nodes.description: Human intestinal enteroid monolayers derived from pluripotent stem cell-derived organoids with NEUROG3 loss, cultured on Transwell filters to quantify permeability and inflammatory-cytokine-sensitive epithelial barrier responses.

evidence.reference_title: Enteroendocrine cells regulate intestinal barrier permeability.
evidence.snippet: seeded human intestinal enteroids with genetic loss of enteroendocrine cells on Transwell filters and evaluated transepithelial electrical resistance, paracellular permeability, and the localization and abundance of junctional proteins.

evidence.reference_title: Enteroendocrine cells regulate intestinal barrier permeability.
evidence.snippet: These findings support a novel role for enteroendocrine cells in augmenting epithelial barrier function in the presence of inflammatory stimuli and present an opportunity for developing therapies to improve the intestinal barrier.

nodes.description: Chronic inflammation leads to fibroblast activation, extracellular matrix deposition, and intestinal strictures.
Overactive IL-23 signaling drives pathogenic Th17 cell differentiation and chronic intestinal inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Innate Immunity Activation in Newly Diagnosed Ileocolonic Crohn's Disease: A Cohort Study.
evidence.snippet: high levels of IL-15 and IL-23 in healthy mucosa suggest that innate immunity is the starter of acute inflammation

nodes.description: Disrupted epithelial integrity and increased permeability permit microbial translocation and amplify mucosal immune activation.

evidence.reference_title: Dysbiotic microbiota interactions in Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Crohn's disease (CD) is a major form of inflammatory bowel disease characterized by transmural inflammation along the alimentary tract. Changes in the microbial composition and reduction in species diversity are recognized as pivotal hallmarks in disease dynamics, challenging the gut barrier function and shaping a pathological immune response in genetically influenced subjects.

experimental_models.description: Supports permeability and barrier-integrity readouts relevant to epithelial dysfunction in Crohn disease.
experimental_models.name: Primary human small-intestinal monolayer barrier model

experimental_models.description: Directly measures transepithelial resistance and paracellular permeability in cytokine-sensitive intestinal epithelial monolayers.
experimental_models.name: Enteroendocrine-deficient intestinal enteroid barrier model

nodes.description: Active intestinal inflammation produces epithelial and stromal injury that disrupts mucosal function in Crohn disease.

evidence.reference_title: Dysbiotic microbiota interactions in Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Crohn's disease (CD) is a major form of inflammatory bowel disease characterized by transmural inflammation along the alimentary tract. Changes in the microbial composition and reduction in species diversity are recognized as pivotal hallmarks in disease dynamics, challenging the gut barrier function and shaping a pathological immune response in genetically influenced subjects.

evidence.reference_title: The landscape of immune dysregulation in Crohn's disease revealed through single-cell transcriptomic profiling in the ileum and colon.
evidence.snippet: Our integrated datasets revealed organ- and compartment-specific responses to acute and chronic inflammation; most immune changes were in cell composition, whereas transcriptional changes dominated among epithelial and stromal cells.

experimental_models.description: Models inflammatory-cytokine-associated epithelial barrier injury in a Crohn-relevant gut epithelium context.
experimental_models.name: Enteroendocrine-deficient intestinal enteroid barrier model

evidence.reference_title: Duodenal Crohn's Disease-a Diagnostic Conundrum.
evidence.snippet: The most common phenotype is stricturing disease which can lead to obstructive-like symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Intestinal stricture in Crohn's disease: A 2020 update.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 70% of patients inevitably develop fibrosis-associated intestinal stricture after 10 years of CD diagnosis, which seriously affects their quality of life.

evidence.reference_title: Endoscopic Management of Colonic Obstruction.
evidence.snippet: Benign etiologies of colonic obstructions include...inflammatory processes such as Crohn's disease.

nodes.description: Impaired autophagy in lamina propria macrophages (particularly with hyperactive LRRK2 variants such as G2019S, N2081D) leads to defective clearance of intracellular bacteria and pro-inflammatory cytokine release that impairs Paneth cell homeostasis.

evidence.reference_title: Macrophage LRRK2 hyperactivity impairs autophagy and induces Paneth cell dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: LRRK2-mediated pro-inflammatory cytokine release from phagocytes impaired Paneth cell function, which was rescued by LRRK2 kinase inhibition through activation of autophagy

evidence.reference_title: Macrophage LRRK2 hyperactivity impairs autophagy and induces Paneth cell dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: patients with CD and mice carrying hyperactive LRRK2 polymorphisms developed Paneth cell dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: complete assessment involves laboratory abnormalities, including micronutrient deficiencies

evidence.reference_title: Weight loss from diagnosis of Crohn's disease to one year post-diagnosis results in earlier surgery.
evidence.snippet: Malnutrition might play a key role in the prognosis of patients with Crohn's disease (CD)

nodes.description: Alterations in gut microbiota contribute to the disease mechanisms.

evidence.reference_title: Dysbiotic microbiota interactions in Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Crohn's disease (CD) is a major form of inflammatory bowel disease characterized by transmural inflammation along the alimentary tract. Changes in the microbial composition and reduction in species diversity are recognized as pivotal hallmarks in disease dynamics, challenging the gut barrier function and shaping a pathological immune response in genetically influenced subjects.

evidence.reference_title: Crohn's disease--defect in innate defence.
evidence.snippet: This ileal and colonic defect in innate defence mediated by a deficiency of the protective alpha- and beta-defensins may enable the luminal microbes to invade the mucosa and trigger the inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Nutrigenetics, nutrigenomics and inflammatory bowel diseases.
evidence.snippet: Inflammatory bowel disease includes ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, which are both inflammatory disorders of the gastrointestinal tract. Both types of inflammatory bowel disease have a complex etiology, resulting from a genetically determined susceptibility interacting with environmental factors, including the diet and gut microbiota.

experimental_models.description: Supports controlled host-microbiota interface experiments relevant to dysbiosis-linked epithelial dysfunction in Crohn disease.
experimental_models.name: Primary human small-intestinal monolayer barrier model

nodes.description: Chemokine-driven infiltration of macrophages and other myeloid cells into perianal tissues, establishing chronic inflammation.
nodes.id: Myeloid Cell Recruitment to Perianal Tissue

nodes.description: Interferon-driven macrophage activation promotes fibroblast activation and matrix degradation, creating tissue disruption that leads to fistula tract formation.

Human pluripotent stem cell-derived intestinal organoids combined with matched PSC-derived macrophages to model resident myeloid-epithelial interactions and inflammatory cytokine responses in intestinal tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Deriving Human Intestinal Organoids with Functional Tissue-Resident Macrophages All From Pluripotent Stem Cells.
evidence.snippet: HIOs and macrophages were generated separately through the directed differentiation of human pluripotent stem cells and combined in vitro.

evidence.reference_title: Deriving Human Intestinal Organoids with Functional Tissue-Resident Macrophages All From Pluripotent Stem Cells.
evidence.snippet: This new organoid system can be used to investigate the molecular mechanisms involved in inflammatory bowel disease.

nodes.description: Defective autophagy in Paneth cells due to mutations in autophagy genes (ATG16L1, ATG5) causes abnormal granule formation and impaired antimicrobial peptide secretion.

evidence.reference_title: A key role for autophagy and the autophagy gene Atg16l1 in mouse and human intestinal Paneth cells.
evidence.snippet: ATG16L1- and ATG5-deficient Paneth cells exhibited notable abnormalities in the granule exocytosis pathway

evidence.reference_title: Perianal Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Perianal Crohn's disease can manifest as skin tags, ulcers, fissures, abscesses, fistulas or stenoses.

evidence.reference_title: Fistulizing Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Fistulas are common in Crohn's disease. A population-based study has shown a cumulative risk of 33% after 10 years and 50% after 20 years. Perianal fistulas were the most common (54%).

evidence.reference_title: A child presents with perianal symptoms - how often is this Crohn's disease?
evidence.snippet: Of children presenting with a perianal symptom, three percent will eventually be diagnosed with CD. At highest risk (35%) were males aged 10 years or older with a perianal fistula.

nodes.description: Polarized primary human small-intestinal epithelial monolayers cultured on Transwells to study barrier integrity, permeability, and host-microbiota interface biology relevant to inflammatory bowel disease.
nodes.id: Primary human small-intestinal monolayer barrier model

evidence.reference_title: Nonsteroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drug-Induced Leaky Gut Modeled Using Polarized Monolayers of Primary Human Intestinal Epithelial Cells.
evidence.snippet: The results we outline here establish the utility of this novel platform, representative of the human small intestinal epithelium, to understand NSAID toxicity, which can be applied to study multiple aspects of gut barrier function including defense against infectious pathogens and host-microbiota interactions.

nodes.description: Tumor necrosis factor-like ligand 1A (TL1A) activates T cells through death receptor 3 (DR3), promoting inflammatory cytokine production and recruitment of myeloid cells to sites of tissue damage.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of Crohn's disease.
evidence.snippet: Patients may experience diarrhea, abdominal pain, fever, weight loss, abdominal masses, and anemia.

evidence.reference_title: Crohn's disease in childhood.
evidence.snippet: abdominal pain, diarrhoea, and weight loss were the common presenting symptoms
